growth_chart.projection_basis: Possible future value one year out: today's $149.75 account value compounded at the CLU 1.25%/mo target → ~$174 by 2027-06, performance-only with no new deposits. Confidence is low — only ~1 week of live history, and a $100 mid-week deposit dominated week-1 value (underlying market performance ≈ -$1.5; the TWR return_index sits at 0.975, i.e. ~-2.5% net of deposits). The site recomputes this 12-month trajectory from the latest account value × the CLU monthly target each Mon/Wed/Fri close as deposit-free history accumulates. Layer contributions on top with the Added-Funds Sim.

next_decisions.thesis: DRIP is the most-underweight tier (0% vs 30% Accumulation target) and the top priority for the $36.37 cash. Double-blocked: the dividend engine emits broken data (§15.3 unverifiable) and SAFE MODE forbids entries.
next_decisions.trigger: 6:00 AM Daily Brief — route to DRIP only if a fresh matrix lifts SAFE MODE AND dividend data is reliable; else hold cash

LAB at 25.7% is over its 20% staging cap and dilutes via base growth — no force-sell (§17.3). Gate bar stays 3/5 until there is enough graded data to justify tightening.

intelligence_evolution.summary: Established the 3-tier framework and first positions across semis + innovation ETF, then built the CORE tier 0%→50% by routing deferred idle cash (VOO/QQQ/SOXX). Live signal engine came online (34 tickers) but proved intermittent — it missed the 5:50 AM and 3:20 PM runs on Monday, triggering SAFE MODE twice. Completed the first full stop-loss cycle (MRVL -5.71%) and recalibrated LAB stops to ATR-based sizing.
intelligence_evolution.key_insight: Three systems validated under fire: (1) the stop-loss loop executed end-to-end on a fractional position via agentic market sell, (2) the compound entry gate caught an overbought false-promote (PANW), and (3) the §2.5 staleness guard correctly halted trading on a stale matrix — twice in one day. The recurring risk is now operational (engine cron reliability), not strategic.
intelligence_evolution.next_phase: Phase 2 — Pattern Recognition (target: 10 sessions, 3+ medium/high patterns, first signal-conditioned LAB grades vs QQQ on 07-01/07-02)

strategy_dna.note: Hard guardrail. Never evaluated, never overridden.
Enforced pre-trade via get_portfolio. No exceptions.
LAB entries require the earnings block + DO_NOT_TRADE check, then >=3/5 quant criteria. Reversed PANW's thesis-only promotion.
Triggered twice Monday (5:50 AM + 3:20 PM engine misses). No new entries on stale signals — managed existing positions only.

strategy_dna.rule: Tiered Stops (LAB ATR-based / CORE + DRIP none)
strategy_dna.note: LAB fractional positions use manual/agentic market-sell checks each session at their ATR stop_pct (INTC 12.6 / ARKK 5.2 / NVDA 5.0). CORE/DRIP carry no stop (§13/§15.4).
New/idle capital routes to the most underweight tier vs stage targets (DRIP > ETF > Stock tiebreak). $75 idle cash → CORE (0%→50%). DRIP (-30pp) is next.
Deposit-free return chained each Mon/Wed/Fri close. Internal reallocations (cash→ETF) excluded. Index at 0.975 after Monday's +0.5% markup.
New entries at the 6:00 AM and 10:45 AM windows only. After-Hours is analysis/planning — no new entries.
MRVL→SOXX (done, stop-out), ARKK→QQQ (+10%), INTC→SOXX (+15%). Shifting the book toward an ETF-anchored model to cut single-name noise.

risk_profile.notes: Cash buffer normalized to 24.3% after deploying $75 to CORE. The book is now 6 positions across LAB (3 single names) + CORE (3 broad/sector ETFs), but deployed capital is still semi-heavy: SOXX + INTC + NVDA ≈ $50 (~33% of the account) sit in semiconductors. SOXX diversifies the single-name risk. DRIP (0%) is the missing leg — building it is the next diversification step, blocked on the dividend-engine fix.

weekly_recap.summary: Week 2 in progress (Mon close). The week opened by deploying the $111 dry powder: $75 routed to CORE (VOO/QQQ/SOXX $25 ea) building the tier 0%→50%, $36 held for DRIP. Monday's relief rally marked the LAB sleeve up (+0.5% deposit-free; ARKK +4.2% the best). The signal engine missed two scheduled runs (5:50 AM + 3:20 PM), forcing two SAFE-MODE sessions, so the After-Hours took no new action. DRIP remains blocked on the dividend-engine data bug. No trades graded yet — first signal-conditioned LAB grades land 07-01/07-02 vs QQQ.
